data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Flat after S&P 500 Top 7,000 for the First Time
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were steady on Thursday evening after the S&P 500 Index (SPX) achieved its first-ever close above 7,000 during the regular session. Futures on the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) and the S&P 500 were up 0.12% and 0.05%, respectively, at 6:16 p.m. EDT on April 16, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) futures were down 0.03%.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

In the regular trading session, major stock indices closed higher as investors stayed optimistic about a potential resolution to the Middle East conflict, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite both setting fresh records. The Nasdaq, S&P 500, and Dow rose 0.4%, 0.3%, and 0.2%, respectively.

Looking ahead, Ally Financial (ALLY), Truist Financial (TFC), State Street (STT), Autoliv (ALV), and Regions Financial (RF) are scheduled to report quarterly numbers tomorrow.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Here's Why Microsoft (MSFT) Stock Rallied Today – April 16, 2026
data.description:
Shares of tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) rallied on Thursday, extending a strong multi-day rally, after CEO Satya Nadella shared a key update. He announced that the company's Fairweather data center in Wisconsin is now up and running ahead of schedule. This development adds to the stock's recent momentum, as it is now in the middle of one of its strongest rallies in years.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Unsurprisingly, Nadella emphasized how large and powerful the new facility is by calling it the most advanced AI data center in the world. The site is built to connect hundreds of thousands of GB200 chips into one unified system. Because of this design, it will play an important role in supporting Microsoft's growing AI needs. Impressively, the data center sits on more than 300 acres in Mt. Pleasant, Wisconsin, and is expected to reach 3.3 gigawatts of capacity by late 2027.

On top of that, the Wisconsin site is part of a wider network of connected data centers across the U.S. For instance, Microsoft previously launched a similar Fairweather facility in Atlanta, and both locations are linked through a dedicated high-speed network. This allows data to move faster between sites, which is critical for large AI workloads, and supports Microsoft's long-term AI strategy. It is also helping drive the stock's recent 12.5% gain over the past five trading sessions, its strongest run since 2020.

What Is the Price Target for Microsoft?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 35 Buys and three Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average MSFT price target of $571.29 per share implies 36.2% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Will Microsoft Stock (MSFT) Continue Its Rally? William Power Weighs In
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) stock might finally be turning a corner after a rough period. Concerns that AI could undermine traditional software, along with unease over surging CapEx and a heavy reliance on the loss-making OpenAI for a sizable share of its backlog, drove shares down by more than 30%. The stock has since rallied ~18% from its late-March lows, hinting a floor may be forming, though it still sits 13% lower for the year.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Against this backdrop, with the tech giant due to report FQ3 (March quarter) results on April 29, 5-star Baird analyst William Power thinks the setup is appealing.

Although Microsoft is exposed to risks in parts of its app portfolio, Power thinks its broad global footprint, strong enterprise ties, and "deep integrations" give it a relative edge over many other established SaaS players.

"While we understand investors' frustrations pertaining to arguably slower pace of innovation vs. the AI Labs (particularly Anthropic), MSFT also continues to innovate and enterprise reach is a key advantage," the 5-star analyst went on to say.

Power notes that investors remain uncertain about Copilot's positioning amid "accelerating innovation" from Anthropic's Claude and OpenAI's ChatGPT. While this creates some risk, Copilot benefits from deep integration within the M365 suite and Microsoft's strong enterprise reach. Moreover, Judson Althoff, CEO of MSFT Commercial, noted earlier this month that Copilot achieved its ambitious FQ3 targets.

Meanwhile, Azure remains a "key growth pillar," with Power expecting FQ3 growth at 39% (37% ex-FX), in line with the guide. Demand continues to exceed available supply, an issue seen across the industry, which is likely driving ongoing increases in capital expenditures but Power views that "as a good problem to have." However, supply constraints and internal investment priorities might limit Azure's ability to reaccelerate growth this quarter, although expanding data center capacity could "unlock higher growth exiting this year."

As for the raw results, Power expects a "solid quarter overall." The analyst anticipates total revenue of $81.2 billion, representing 15.9% year-over-year growth and broadly in line with the guide, with the potential for modest upside similar to recent quarters. Guidance includes an approximately 3% y/y FX tailwind, though FX could ultimately contribute 300–350bps in FQ3, implying slight upside to reported figures. Power forecasts Microsoft Cloud gross margins at 65% (down from 67% in FQ2) and GAAP operating margins at 45.1%, resulting in adjusted EPS of $4.06, roughly in line with consensus.

So, what does this ultimately all mean for investors? Power assigns MSFT shares an Outperform (i.e., Buy) rating, although his price target goes from $540 to $500, now suggesting the stock will gain 19% over the one-year timeframe. (To watch Power's track record, click here)

34 other analysts join Power in the bull camp, while 3 additional Holds can't detract from a Strong Buy consensus rating. Going by the $571.29 average price target, a year from now, the shares will be changing hands for a ~36% premium. (See MSFT stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: AMD and Intel (INTC): Why This Top Analyst Is Raising Price Targets on These Two Chip Stocks
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Intel (INTC), two leading semiconductor companies, saw their price targets raised by Bernstein, reflecting improving trends in data centers and AI demand. Top Bernstein analyst Stacy Rasgon lifted the price target on AMD to $265 (from $235) and on Intel to $60 (from $36), while maintaining Market Perform ratings on both stocks.

data.title: Sazerac's $15B Bid Intensifies Takeover Battle for Brown-Forman — What Does It Mean for BF.B Stock?
data.description:
U.S. private spirits giant Sazerac has offered to acquire rival Brown-Forman (BF.B), one of the world's largest spirits and wine companies, for $15 billion, according to multiple media reports.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

BF.B stock rose about 3% early Thursday following the news, even as analysts have laid out a bullish case for a potential takeover of the Jack Daniel's maker.

According to the Wall Street Journal, which first broke the news, the offer amounts to $32 a share. This represents an 8.2% premium to Brown-Forman's closing price of $29.57 on Wednesday.

Sazerac, Pernod Ricard Battle for Brown-Forman

The bid from Sazerac, which joined the takeover race earlier this month, puts to the test efforts by French spirits giant Pernod Ricard (PDRDF) to merge with Brown-Forman. This comes as Brown-Forman late last month confirmed that it was indeed engaged in acquisition discussions with Pernod Ricard.

The Kentucky-based company noted that if an arrangement is agreed upon with the Jameson maker and clears regulatory hurdles, it "would be akin to a merger of equals, drawing from the talent and expertise of both companies, and creating value for shareholders."

The acquisition interest comes as U.S. tariffs have heaped pressure on alcohol companies, creating worries about demand. However, Brown-Forman last month reaffirmed its forecast for sales this year, even as it beat analysts' estimates for its third-quarter revenue.

What Analysts Think of a Brown-Forman Merger

Lately, analysts lifted their ratings on BF.B, noting upside from a potential merger. For instance, Citi recently upgraded BF.B from Sell to Hold, noting that the whiskey maker holds "transformational merger potential." The banking giant's analyst Filippo Falorni on Wednesday further reaffirmed the Hold rating and set a price target of $31, implying about 5% upside.

JPMorgan analyst Drew Levine also recently made the exact rating move, noting that a prospective merger-and-acquisition deal will support Brown-Forman shares for now as pressure remains on its finances. However, while Levine raised the price target on BF.B from $25 to $27, this implies about 9% downside risk.

Is BF.B Stock a Buy?

Across Wall Street, Brown-Forman's shares remain a Hold based on analysts' consensus rating. This breaks down to one Buy, seven Holds, and two Sells issued over the past three months.

Moreover, the average BF.B price target of $27.94 implies about 6% downside potential.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Ahead of Tesla's Q1 Earnings on April 22, Let's Look at Who Owns TSLA Stock
data.description:
Tesla's (TSLA) stock has declined about 14% year-to-date amid concerns about weak deliveries, lack of innovation, intense competition, and lackluster demand in the electric vehicle (EV) market amid macro uncertainty. The EV maker is scheduled to announce its Q1 2026 earnings on April 22. Wall Street expects Tesla to report earnings per share (EPS) of $0.36 for Q1 2026, reflecting 33% year-over-year growth. Revenue is projected to rise 15.4% to $22.32 billion from the prior-year quarter.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Trade TSLA with leverage

Wall Street is divided on Tesla stock. While Tesla bulls remain optimistic about its full self-driving (FSD) technology, robotaxis, and other AI ventures, several analysts are concerned about the persistent weakness in the EV business.  

Now, according to TipRanks' Ownership Tool, public companies and individual investors own 38.80% of Tesla. They are followed by insiders, exchange-traded funds (ETFs), mutual funds, and other institutional investors at 24.95%, 23.82%, 12.36%, and 0.07%, respectively.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: NFLX Earnings: Netflix Stock Falls despite Upbeat Q1 Results, Chair Reed Hastings Steps Down
data.description:
Shares of streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) fell in after-hours trading despite reporting upbeat results for the first quarter of 2026. Alongside the earnings release, Netflix disclosed that its co‑founder and Chairman Reed Hastings will leave the company's board in June 2026, ending his 29‑year run at the company. Q1 revenue rose 16% year-over-year to $12.3 billion and also beat expectations of $12.18 billion, thanks to stronger‑than‑expected membership additions, higher pricing, and favorable currency movements.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Further, Netflix's earnings per share came in at $1.23, which beat analysts' consensus estimate of $0.76 per share and climbed 86.4% year-over-year. The jump was boosted by the $2.8 billion termination fee tied to the Warner Bros. (WBD) transaction, recognized in "interest and other income."

Netflix's Guidance for Q2 2026

Looking forward, management has provided the following guidance for the second quarter of 2026. Netflix expects 13% revenue growth in Q2 and an operating margin of 32.6%, slightly below last year due to heavier first‑half content amortization tied to title timing. Margin expansion is expected to resume in Q3 and Q4.

Also, the company reaffirmed its full‑year guidance:

  • Netflix's 2026 revenue is expected to be in the range of $50.7 billion to $51.7 billion (12%-14% growth). Analysts expect 2026 sales of $51.38 billion.
  • Operating margin of 31.5%, up from 29.5% in 2025
  • A "rough doubling" of ad revenue
  • Free cash flow is expected to be about $12.5 billion, up from the prior $11 billion estimate.

Is NFLX Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on NFLX stock based on 30 Buys and nine Holds assigned in the past three months. Further, the average NFLX price target of $116.00 per share implies 8.19% upside potential. However, it's worth noting that estimates will likely change following today's earnings report.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Stock Market Today: S&P 500 Sector Leaders and Losers, 4/16/26
data.description:
It was another strong day for the S&P 500 (SPX), as the benchmark index secured a new intraday record high of 7,051. Let's break down the sectors behind the performance.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

Energy was the best-performing sector on Thursday as Brent crude oil futures (BZ) climbed 3%. Despite easing geopolitical tensions, the Strait of Hormuz remains at a near standstill, choking the supply of oil. With higher oil prices, energy producers and refiners are able to benefit from improved margins and stronger revenue expectations.

Several energy stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • APA (APA)
  • EQT (EQT)
  • Valero Energy (VLO)
  • ConocoPhillips (COP)
  • Exxon Mobil (XOM)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, the healthcare sector faced the largest drawdown as investors rotated capital into growth stocks and pivoted away from defensive positioning. Abbott Laboratories (ABT) weighed on the sector after lowering its full-year EPS guidance to reflect the dilutive effects from its acquisition of Exact Sciences (EXAS).

Notable healthcare stocks trading lower include:

  • Abbott Laboratories (ABT)
  • Revvity (RVTY)
  • IQVIA (IQV)
  • Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO)
  • Biogen (BIIB)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Retail Investors Missed the Rally. Is It Too Late to Buy?
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) has surged 11% since March 30 amid easing U.S.-Iran tensions. Retail investors were caught off guard by the V-shaped rally and were largely on the sidelines, said Fundstrat founder Tom Lee. "This is all the makings of the most hated V-shaped rally again," he said.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

However, plenty of upside could be left in the tank. Wall Street has an average S&P 500 year-end price target of 7,607, implying upside of 8% from current levels.

Wall Street Remains Optimistic

Oppenheimer has the highest price target on the street at 8,100, while Bank of America leads to the downside at 7,100.

Lee notes that retail investors will act as "fuel for the continued upside" as the market climbs higher. In addition, Lee expects elevated spending from the war to benefit the economy, pointing out that S&P 500 earnings estimates have continued to accelerate.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Extend Gains as Trump Teases New U.S.-Iran Talks; Israel and Lebanon Agree to a Ceasefire
data.description:
The S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed higher on Thursday, with both ETFs clinching new intraday all-time highs.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • President Trump said the next round of U.S.-Iran peace talks could occur "maybe over the weekend," adding that the U.S. is "very close to making a deal." He warned that fighting could resume if a deal cannot be reached.
  • Pakistani officials expect the U.S. and Iran to reach a "major breakthrough" in nuclear negotiations, sources familiar with the situation told Al Jazeera.
  • Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10-day ceasefire, with Trump inviting Lebanese President Joseph Aoun and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to a gathering in Washington, D.C.
  • Initial jobless claims fell by 11,000 to 207,000, below the consensus estimate of 213,000. Continuing jobless claims increased by 31,000 to 1.818 million and were slightly above the expectation of 1.81 million.
  • Microsoft (MSFT) closed higher after CEO Satya Nadella shared that the company's Fairweather data center in Wisconsin is now operational ahead of schedule.
  • AMD (AMD) advanced to a record high alongside a broad rally in the semiconductor sector.
  • Charles Schwab (SCHW) plummeted following a disappointing earnings report.
  • Oil climbed higher, with Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Chevron (CVX) posting gains.
data.author: Eddie Pan
